question: The frozen-modulus SU(2)-Higgs model on a four-dimensional lattice at gauge coupling beta=2.3 is defined by S_inf = sum_x (1/4) * kappa_1 * Tr[Phi_dag(x) U_mu(x) Phi(x+mu)] + (beta/4) * sum_P Tr U_P, where Phi_dag Phi=1 (fixed modulus). An extended action adds a next-to-nearest-neighbor gauge-Higgs hopping term with coupling kappa_2. Monte Carlo simulations on L^4 lattices (L up to 24) measure energy histograms, latent heats, and Binder cumulant minima for various kappa_1 values along the Confinement-Higgs phase boundary. What is the ORDER of the Confinement-Higgs phase transition of the frozen-modulus SU(2)-Higgs model at kappa_2=0 (standard action)? Answer in JSON with field "transition_order" using one of: "first_order", "continuous", "bkt".

metadata.system: Frozen-modulus SU(2)-Higgs model on 4D L^4 lattice at beta=2.3, Confinement-Higgs phase boundary
metadata.source_excerpt: The combined evidence from the extended-parameter study (a connected line of first-order transitions for kappa_2>0 that weakens continuously as kappa_2->0), the measured nonzero latent heats along that line, and the finite-size specific-heat and Binder cumulant behavior, indicates that the Confinement-Higgs transition for the standard action is consistent with a first-order transition, albeit extremely weak and transient on accessible lattices
metadata.difficulty_note: Extremely weak first-order at kappa_2=0; two-peak distributions not resolved up to L=24 for standard action; first-order inferred from connected kappa_2>0 line and asymptotic Binder cumulant analysis.
